transcript.text: Hello. This video is what I think about Kryon. I've been watching Kryon the last couple of days. I downloaded it off of somebody's YouTube channel, where Lee Carroll, the guy who channels Kryon, talks for a couple of hours.

And I really like Kryon. Kryon of magnetic circle or magnetic surface or something. I think he's different from Bashar and Jane Roberts and some other channels I've heard and Pleiadians because he doesn't really say specific things, tell specific stories.

He doesn't really answer specific questions. He kind of just goes up there and babbles, singing basically a song of, oh, it's so beautiful that we have arrived at ascension, but can't you hear the angels singing?

But he did say some very interesting things. He said the same thing that Bashar did. Bashar's talking about how when you change yourself, if you decide to suddenly become not a drug addict anymore, then you were never a drug addict.
And Kryon was describing that. I'll go back and I'll listen to it again and think if I ever have another revelation, I will know.
Or if I understand something that Kryon says that I didn't include in this video, I'll know it and then I'll make sure to upload another video.
But he said that when you change an attitude of yourself about yourself, then you have changed the past also.
So he was saying that you can change the past.
Like, so he likened it to a horse track. You're going around a track and here in third dimension, if you change yourself, then you only change yourself from this point forward.
But everything from before when you change yourself didn't change.
But if you're like in a higher dimension and you change yourself, automatically the whole track has changed and the past has changed.
So, um, how could you change your past in the third dimension?
For example, how could you, oh, and he also said that, um, well, actually that's another thing.
Yeah, so like change your past, like stop yourself from getting cancer in the past.
Um, maybe you, and like say you're right about to die in this life because you got cancer.
How would you make it so that you never got cancer?
Um, maybe you atone for yourself and the cancer.
Maybe you, you, you, you forgive yourself for getting cancer.
You stop worrying about getting cancer.
You accept getting cancer and dying, um, so that you never, you don't feel the, uh, the cancer having a negative effect on you.
Um, so that you don't have to get cancer again.
And the next time through in this exact life, um, bring, you know, this is bringing into account the idea of parallel universes on an infinite amount of parallel universes.
And this timeline, an infinite amount of parallel universes on an infinite number of different timelines.
So you'd have a parallel universe with that's five seconds behind you that are always communicating with each other.
So you would communicate with the parallel universe where you're living and it's at, and you're a younger age and you haven't gotten cancer yet.
And since you forgave yourself in this life and you decided that you didn't need the cancer in this life, it made it so that you didn't need to have it in the next life.
Um, I don't know if that makes sense, but, um, so then that's how changing, that's, that's how you change the past.
So, um, and maybe you can do that with family members too.
Like, if you don't want one of your parents to die of cancer, then you can change the past by, um, um, accepting the life that you have now, that you're living now.
And accepting it's, it's worth and value and, um, realizing that in another world, they didn't die of cancer and that other world, um, can feel it.
And you, uh, so you actually bring that other parallel universe more into reality.
And, uh, so maybe that's how you, how you, uh, change the past.
That's as good as I can do about that.
If anybody has any idea of what I'm talking about, maybe they can improve on that concept.
But, uh, another interesting thing that Kryon said is that, um, he brought up the concept of, um, non-locality.
Um, where, and I guess, um, that's actually...
Warrants its own video.
But non-locality is, uh, actually something that scientists have, uh, have, um, proven with, uh, with, uh, tests.
Where if you get, if you take a cell out of somebody's arm body without other blood, put it in a Petri dish or whatever, keep it alive with the DNA in there.
And then you take another one out with that same person and take it to another part of the country, 3,000 miles away.
And then you tell the person to think happy thoughts.
And, uh, if you tell them to fear anger and fear, at that very moment, their cell, 3,000 miles away, will, uh, shrivel up.
Uh, and, uh, supposedly that's an actual experiment you can do.
And, um, I would love to try and see if we try and do that experiment myself.
One of these days when I have the time, I'm going to purchase all of the equipment to do all these kinds of experiments.

But, um, but, um, but Kryon was saying that you can, uh, that, that DNA in cells, um, are not only, oh, don't only communicate non, non-locally like that, instantaneously, non-locally, um, within the same person, but within the same family members.
So, like, a mother, if a mother feels, you know, happiness, then the DNA of her child will open up, kind of, like that, at that very moment.
So, you can, you can, and, and the exact, um, uh, example that Kryon said was that you can make your child, um, you can prevent your child from getting cancer from your own thoughts.
And it makes sense by, by, if you live with your child, and if you're happy, then you would make your child happier, and then they wouldn't create the cancer within yourself.
But I think he was even saying that you can cure, miraculously cure your child of cancer by curing yourself.
And then, because you're connected non-locally, you could actually cause miracles in somebody else.
So, I guess that kind of brings in prayer, like mass prayer, like Richard Hoagland said when everybody prayed for him when he had his heart attack in 99.
He, he, he felt the, uh, he felt all of that love.
So, um, anyways, um, so maybe that's how, how non-locality and, um, instantaneously changing the past, how Kryon was saying it, can be manifested in this world.
As you, um, really, mind over matter, you think, you can believe, um, starts in the beliefs, and then, um, and then is manifested instantaneously in the body.
So, uh, yeah, that's pretty much all I remember from Kryon, though.

He pretty much just sings the song of how, uh, how it's pretty much clear and sure and 100% that humanity is going to ascend to the next level of, um, of, uh, development and spirituality and everything.
And, uh, yeah, it's, uh, it's very uplifting to listen to and the kind of thing you can listen to over and over again that you probably should listen to over and over again.
So, there you go.
